description:
You are working inside an Apptainer-based development container as a DevOps / container-orchestration specialist.  
    A tiny SQLite database located at  
        /home/user/microservices/logs.db  
    is already populated by upstream micro-services.  
    Your manager wants a *single* one-off command line (no intermediate scripts) that will:

    1. Open /home/user/microservices/logs.db with SQLite.
    2. Count how many rows in the table named “services” have the column “status” equal to the string “running”.
    3. Write that count, **and nothing else**, to a brand-new file  
           /home/user/output/services_status.log

    Very strict output format (this is what the automated grader will look for):

        Running services: <COUNT>

    • Replace <COUNT> with the numeric count returned by your query.  
    • The line must be terminated by a UNIX newline (LF).  
    • No extra spaces, blank lines, or other characters are allowed.  

    Important notes for success:

    • Perform everything from the command line (one command is enough; shell operators such as redirection are allowed).  
    • Do **not** change the existing database. Only read from it.  
    • Make sure the destination directory /home/user/output already exists (create it if necessary) before writing the log.  
    • After running your command, you can cat the file to visually confirm the correct single-line output.  
    • The automated test will open /home/user/output/services_status.log and expect exactly the line above with the correct count and newline.

    Once finished, display the contents of /home/user/output/services_status.log in the terminal so that a human reviewer can also see the result.
truth:
Initial file system state expected **before** the agent starts:

    1. Directory: /home/user/microservices/
    2. Existing SQLite database: /home/user/microservices/logs.db

       Schema inside logs.db:

           CREATE TABLE services (
               id     INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
               name   TEXT    NOT NULL,
               status TEXT    NOT NULL
           );

       Pre-loaded rows:

           1 | auth-api      | running
           2 | payments-api  | stopped
           3 | user-api      | running
           4 | metrics       | running
           5 | gateway       | stopped

       Thus, there are exactly 3 rows where status = 'running'.

    3. Directory /home/user/output/ is *absent* at container start so that the agent needs to create it (mkdir -p).  
       No files exist under /home/user/output/.

    Final state that the test suite will verify **after** the agent finishes:

    • The file /home/user/output/services_status.log must exist.  
    • File permissions: 0644 (automatically fine if created normally).  
    • File content must be exactly (including trailing LF):

          Running services: 3\n

    (3 represents the truth value derived from the current contents of the database.)

    • No other modifications to /home/user/microservices/logs.db are allowed (checksum will be verified).
